name: Children's Allergy and Asthma Data Repository (CADRE)
description: The Children's Allergy and Asthma Data Repository (CADRE) is a centralized resource created to address a critical gap in asthma research: identifying the risk factors and biological mechanisms that lead to childhood asthma. By uniting data from various cohorts, CADRE empowers researchers to uncover insights that individual studies alone cannot provide. CADRE brings together data from NIH funded allergy and asthma cohort studies to harmonize data for future collaborative studies, leveraging data collected through the Children's Respiratory and Environmental Workgroup (CREW). The dataset includes data on 9,600 newborns recruited over a 40+ year time span with broad representation of race/ethnicity, allergy and asthma risk, neighborhood characteristics, and geographic regions.
conditionsOfAccess: Data dictionaries describing the harmonized variables are publicly available for download. Access to the harmonized participant dataset requires contacting the CADRE team via the online contact form at https://redcap.wisc.edu/surveys/?s=AYTTTFACXLP83XYL or by email at gmcmahan@wisc.edu.

funder.name: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID), Division of Allergy Immunology and Transplantation and Office of Data Science and Emerging Technologies
NIH Office of the Director, Office of Data Science Strategy

distribution.name: core_data_elements_csv
distribution.description: Downloadable CSV spreadsheet listing the 200 core harmonized variables used most frequently in CREW and CADRE publications, with descriptions and response options for each variable.

distribution.name: ancillary_data_elements_csv
distribution.description: Downloadable CSV spreadsheet listing additional harmonized variables beyond the core set, organized by dataset, with descriptions and response options.

recordSet.name: core_data_elements
recordSet.description: Records from the CADRE Core Data Elements spreadsheet. Each row describes one harmonized variable, including its name, description, and response options.

field.name: Data_element
field.description: Short name or code for the harmonized variable.

field.name: Description
field.description: Human-readable description of the harmonized variable.

field.name: Response_options
field.description: Allowed values or coding scheme for the variable (e.g. 0=No, 1=Yes, or a list of category labels).

recordSet.name: ancillary_data_elements
recordSet.description: Records from the CADRE Ancillary Data Elements spreadsheet. Each row describes one harmonized variable that has been used less frequently than the core set, organized by dataset.

field.name: Dataset
field.description: Name of the dataset or data domain to which this variable belongs (e.g. Aeroallergen).

field.name: Dataset_ordinal
field.description: Ordinal position of the variable within its dataset.

field.name: Data_element
field.description: Short name or code for the harmonized variable.

field.name: Description
field.description: Human-readable description of the harmonized variable.

field.name: Response_options
field.description: Allowed values or coding scheme for the variable.
